title: receipts project config
description: Project-specific plumbing for the receipts gates. Produced by `receipts init` (detect -> confirm -> write); rarely hand-authored. The gate LOGIC ships generic; this file only tells receipts how to build/test/reach THIS project and what marks a fix-claim.

claim.description: How a PR claims to fix an issue, and how it honestly downgrades.

issue_link.description: Regex matching how a PR links the issue it fixes.

require_receipt_for.description: When the enforcer requires a receipt. 'issue-link' (default): only a fix-claim (a PR matching issue_link) must carry one - a PR with no issue link and no work-type passes untouched. 'any-source-change' (strict): a PR that changes production source (excluding docs / tests / CI / config) must ALSO carry a receipt, an honest downgrade tag, or an explicit work-type - closing the 'omit the issue link => silent green' bypass.

downgrade_tags.description: Tags that mark a PR as NOT claiming a clean fix (the honesty ladder); these pass the gate but are tracked, not treated as 'fixed'.

build.description: How receipts binds to the build under test and (optionally) the deployed app.

sha_source.description: How to confirm which commit the build/deploy carries (G3). 'none' = no deploy; verify against the built artifact + test run.

platform.description: Detected deploy platform (informational), e.g. vercel | railway | netlify | fly | render | none.

deploy_host_patterns.description: Host globs that count as 'pointed at the deployed build' (for optional live-drive).

verify_against.description: Which environment the enforcer re-verifies on (a key of `environments`).

verify.description: How to run the receipt: the carried red->green acceptance test. The PRIMARY verification; uses the project's own test framework + CI auth. Required for the ENFORCER (it errors without test_command); an agent-home config (skills + session cwd, no code repo) may omit this whole block - that is why `verify` is not a top-level required field.

test_command.description: Run ONE carried acceptance test. `{test}` is substituted with the test path/selector.

suite_command.description: Run the full suite (G9 full-scope green: the enforcer runs this on head after the narrow receipt, so a regression in code the changed test never exercises is caught, not just the changed test).

require_fresh_base.description: G8 fresh base: whether the enforcer flags a branch that is behind its base (a green earned on a stale base is green on code that won't ship). 'warn' (default) surfaces it without failing the check; 'block' fails it; 'off' disables.

on_load_error_red.description: What to do when the receipt's RED on base is a load/collection error (import / syntax / no-tests), not an assertion failure - a red that may not prove the symptom (the test may just import code that only exists on head). 'warn' (default): surface it, still allow. 'block': fail it, EXCEPT when the PR is `work-type: feature` (a feature's test is legitimately red until the feature exists).

command_timeout_ms.description: Optional per-command timeout (milliseconds) for the test/suite commands the enforcer runs. A hung test is killed and reported rather than hanging the whole job. 0 / unset = no timeout (the CI job's own timeout still applies).

live_drive.description: OPTIONAL advanced: a command/script that drives the deployed app for symptoms a unit/e2e test can't cover. null = not used (the carried test is enough).

degrade.description: Honest behavior when the symptom cannot be re-verified. Never silent-pass; never block-all.

on_no_receipt.description: What to do when a fix-claim carries no re-runnable acceptance test.

on_unreachable_build.description: What to do when the deployed build can't be reached for re-verification.

gates.description: Which gates apply to this project, and per-gate selectors. Gates are referenced by ID (G0-G10) - the shared vocabulary across the spec, the `gates` skill, the Stop hooks, and the enforcer. `receipts init` writes a safe default (all on); disable the gates that do not fit (e.g. G10 in a single repo with no split deploy, or the deploy-surface gates in a library with no deploy). The skill reads this to know which gates to apply; the enforcer reads it to know which checks to run.

medium.description: The software type, so the gates apply in the right terms - a 'surface' / 'value' / 'terminal action' means something different for a web app vs an API vs a library vs a CLI vs a data pipeline vs infra. A hint the agent reads alongside references/MEDIA.md; free-form and expandable. Common values: web, api, service, library, cli, mobile, desktop, data, ml, infra, database, smart-contract, browser-extension, embedded, game, unknown.

work_type.description: OPTIONAL default work type (fix / feature / migration / refactor / chore). Usually LEFT UNSET - work type is per-PR, not per-project (most repos mix), so the agent determines it per task and a PR signals it with a `work-type:` line in its body. It sets what the receipt asserts (the acceptance criterion), and for refactor/chore it inverts the receipt to 'the full suite stays green' (no behavior change). See references/WORK-TYPES.md.

enabled.description: Which gates run here: "all", or an explicit array of gate IDs (G0-G10).

disabled.description: Gate IDs to turn off even if `enabled` includes them; takes precedence over `enabled`.

G6.description: Surface coverage - the 'sweep the twins' assist. Catches a pattern applied to SOME sibling surfaces but not all (an 'app-wide' change that is actually partial). Two mechanisms: declared `surfaces` (a glob + a required marker, any language) and a built-in JS/TS heuristic (an import rolled out to >=2 siblings flags same-named twins that missed it). Runs on every PR with a diff.

mode.description: What an incomplete rollout does: warn (default) or block.

auto.description: Enable the JS/TS import-rollout heuristic (no config needed). Set false to rely only on declared `surfaces`.

surfaces.description: Declared surface families: each is a set of files (glob) that must all carry a marker (substring). Encodes an 'app-wide' invariant the enforcer re-checks - language-agnostic.

glob.description: Files in the family, e.g. 'src/**/*Table.tsx'. Supports * and **.

marker.description: Substring every family member must contain, e.g. 'Pagination' or 'usePagination' or 'LIMIT'.

always.description: Check coverage on every PR (a standing invariant), not only when a PR touches the marker. Default false.

G7.description: Dependent-test-selection. The enforcer computes the NEW dependents of the changed source (a freshly-added consumer file, or a freshly-added import edge) and re-runs their co-located tests on head, catching an integration regression the carried receipt never exercises. Built-in support: a JS/TS import scan; for any stack, point `graph` at a consumer graph.

mode.description: What a failing NEW-dependent test does: warn (default - the reverse-dependency set is heuristic) or block.

graph.description: Optional path (in the repo, read at head) to a consumer graph JSON: an object mapping each importer file to the list of files it imports. Used to compute dependents for stacks the built-in JS/TS scanner does not cover.

verify_all_dependents.description: Widen the scope from NEW dependents (the default - the freshly-routed consumer) to EVERY consumer of the changed surface. Use for high-blast-radius changes; slower and noisier.

integration_branch.description: The branch whose tip 'fresh base' is measured against (e.g. main, dev-django). The enforcer also reads the PR's base automatically; this documents the intended integration branch for the agent.

G10.description: Rollout-compatibility selectors. The enforcer back-compat-checks changed contract artifacts on a PR (built-in: OpenAPI/Swagger/AsyncAPI/JSON-Schema get a structural breaking-diff; GraphQL/proto/yaml get a 'changed - verify manually' warning).

contract_paths.description: Extra globs of contract artifacts to back-compat-check, on top of the built-in detection (e.g. "api/schema.json", "**/*.proto", "proto/**").

mode.description: What a detected breaking contract change does: warn (default) or block.

contract_pairs.description: Producer/consumer halves whose contract must stay compatible across the deploy window.

agent.description: How the agent-side Stop hooks bind to THIS project: which skills are fix/build loops (watched by the trajectory reminder + carrying the kb touchpoints), what extra by-value-query patterns count, and which tracker statuses mean 'fixed'. The hooks ship generic defaults; these MERGE OVER them (never replace the generics, so a clean install still works). Written by `receipts init`. Deploy-host patterns live in `build.deploy_host_patterns` (shared with the enforcer).

loop_skills.description: Skill names that are fix/build loops: the trajectory-reminder watches these (append-on-exit) and they carry the kb query/append touchpoints. The shipped 'gates' plus any project loop skills.

staging_query_patterns.description: Extra substrings / MCP-tool names that count as a by-value query against the deployed build (e.g. a DB proxy host, a query tool), on top of the generic defaults.

closeout_fixed_statuses.description: Tracker status strings that mean 'claimed fixed' (e.g. 'Fixed - Pending Retest', 'Verified', 'Closed'); the verification gate treats a status update to one of these as a fix close-out.

repo_name.description: Default repo tag for trajectory-kb appends (the agent may override per append).
